sections.biography-source-undefined: Tan Cheng, courtesy name Gongwen, hailed from Qi county in Guanghan commandery. He served as a commandery administrator in many areas and held important local posts. He would serve the court as Minister Steward and Minister of Ceremonies. He was praised in the Huayangguozhi as being “leisurely and content”. He was specifically promoted to Minister of Ceremonies during the administrations of Fei Yi and Jiang Wei because he could “reconcile isolated positions”. In contrast, veteran officials like Lai Min and Meng Guang had stagnated careers. Even though he was younger and had less years of government service than Meng Guang, he attained higher ranks than he ever did. Chen Shou thought that this was because Tan Cheng was not as critical as Meng Guang.

full_text:
Tan Cheng (Gongwen) 鐔承 (公文)
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Tan Cheng (鐔承), styledGongwen (公文), was born in Qi county, Guanghan commandery. He served as commandery administrators and then Minister of Ceremonies. Tan Cheng was affiliated with the Shu Kingdom.

BiographySource Undefined
Tan Cheng, courtesy name Gongwen, hailed from Qi county in Guanghan commandery. He served as a commandery administrator in many areas and held important local posts. He would serve the court as Minister Steward and Minister of Ceremonies. He was praised in theHuayangguozhias being “leisurely and content”. He was specifically promoted to Minister of Ceremonies during the administrations of Fei Yi and Jiang Wei because he could “reconcile isolated positions”. In contrast, veteran officials like Lai Min and Meng Guang had stagnated careers. Even though he was younger and had less years of government service than Meng Guang, he attained higher ranks than he ever did. Chen Shou thought that this was because Tan Cheng was not as critical as Meng Guang.
